scope: 12 contracts (CONT folded into SEQ+PAYLOAD => 11 distinct mutation hooks)

lanes.verilator: verilator-5.046 --assert constrained-random sim of embedded SVA

anti_vacuity: model carries 30 $assert + 8 $cover cells; cover(drop_pulse/a_seq/a_end/...) proven reachable; clean power-on reset assumed
bugs_found_via_cross_check: verilator BADVLTPRAGMA: a TB comment starting with the word 'verilator' was parsed as a pragma -> reworded.
STATUS mutation v1 (drop_count <= drop_count) is an identity NBA -> yosys optimized it to a no-op -> vacuous-looking pass. Caught because the mutant did not fail.

STATUS mutation v2 (later unconditional drop_count <= 0 overriding a conditional +1): yosys formal kept the conditional +1 (NBA-precedence modeling differs from Verilog last-wins) so formal missed it while verilator caught it -> rewrote as a single unambiguous `DROP_INC macro so both lanes catch it.
First formal run had no reset constraint (free init): solver used drop_count=255 so 255+1=0 masked the first STATUS drop -> added a power-on reset assumption.
caveats: Single-context, key simplified to pkt_tag (full key adds src_eid/to; same logic shape).
Payload-data exact ordering is proven only via length accounting + symbolic checks here; full byte-ordering belongs to a scoreboard sim lane.
Formal proofs are from the reset state (power-on reset assumed).
